name: University of North Carolina
notes: 22 NCAA Women's national titles — the most storied women's program in history. Men's program consistently top-15.

name: Stanford University
notes: Academic powerhouse with elite soccer pedigree. Admission is need-based and highly competitive regardless of athletic talent.

name: UCLA
notes: Back-to-back Women's national champion. Men's program has produced numerous MLS and international players.

name: Florida State University
notes: FSU Women are perennial ACC title contenders. Warm weather, strong academics, and top-tier recruiting.

name: Duke University
notes: Elite academics and consistently top-5 women's program. Men's program has won multiple ACC titles. Strong track record sending players to USWNT.

name: University of Virginia
notes: Men's program is a national powerhouse with 7 NCAA titles. Women's program consistently ranked top-10.

name: Georgetown University
notes: Premier Big East program with strong international recruiting. Prepares players for careers beyond soccer with elite academics.

name: University of Notre Dame
notes: Strong faith-based culture. Women's program regularly reaches NCAA tournaments. Campus and facilities are world-class.

name: Penn State University
notes: Women's program is a national title contender under Erica Dambach. Multiple USWNT players have come through Penn State.

name: University of Portland
notes: Small Jesuit school with a big soccer tradition. Women's program won the 2002 NCAA title; consistently ranked nationally.

name: Santa Clara University
notes: Brandi Chastain's alma mater. Top WCC program in a strong soccer region; Jesuit academics with Silicon Valley location.

name: Gonzaga University
notes: Strong WCC program with rising national profile. Jesuit school with excellent academic support and close-knit community.

name: University of Washington
notes: Large public university with competitive Big Ten soccer. Beautiful Pacific Northwest location; strong recruiting pipeline from the Pacific Northwest.

name: Indiana University
notes: Indiana Men's is a historic national powerhouse with 8 NCAA titles. Women's program is consistently competitive in the Big Ten.

name: University of Michigan
notes: Big Ten flagship with strong academics. Both programs compete at the upper tier of the Big Ten annually.

name: Ohio State University
notes: Large Big Ten university with strong alumni network. Both programs are NCAA tournament regulars.

name: University of Wisconsin
notes: Strong academic Big Ten school. Madison is an outstanding college town with a passionate soccer fanbase.

name: Rutgers University
notes: New Jersey flagship with a deep soccer culture. Close proximity to NYC provides strong recruiting and career networking.

name: Northwestern University
notes: Academically elite Big Ten school. If you can handle Northwestern coursework, it opens doors well beyond soccer.

name: University of Maryland
notes: Men's program has won 4 NCAA titles. DC-area location provides strong professional networking and diversity.

name: Wake Forest University
notes: Small ACC school that punches well above its weight in soccer. Strong brotherhood/sisterhood culture and elite academic profile.

name: Clemson University
notes: ACC program with strong fan support and solid soccer tradition. Women's program has been building momentum nationally.

name: NC State University
notes: Research university in Raleigh's tech corridor. Strong STEM focus. More accessible than UNC/Duke academically while still ACC-competitive.

name: University of Pittsburgh
notes: Men's program won the 2023 ACC tournament. Pittsburgh has a passionate sports culture with an urban campus.

name: Boston College
notes: Elite Jesuit school in Boston. Women's program is a consistent NCAA tournament qualifier. Strong alumni network in finance and law.

name: University of Louisville
notes: Women's program has been one of the biggest risers in the ACC over recent years. Strong identity in the basketball-heavy city.

name: Virginia Tech
notes: Engineering powerhouse with solid ACC soccer programs. Blacksburg is a tight-knit college town with passionate athletic culture.

name: University of Texas
notes: Flagship Texas program with major resources. Austin is one of the best college cities in America. Women's program is consistently elite.

name: Baylor University
notes: Christian university with strong academic and athletic culture. Women's program is a consistent Big 12 contender.

name: West Virginia University
notes: Women's program under Nikki Izzo-Brown is one of the most decorated in Big 12 history. Good fit for high-energy, physical players.

name: Texas Christian University
notes: Private school with strong campus culture and rising athletic profile. Fort Worth gives big-city access with a college feel.

name: Brigham Young University
notes: Faith-based school with strong values culture. Women's program has been nationally ranked. No athletic recruiting on Sundays per school policy.

name: University of Kansas
notes: Classic college town with a passionate university community. Women's program is building consistency in a competitive Big 12.

name: University of Colorado
notes: Boulder's natural beauty and outdoor culture make it a top destination for athletes. Rising Big 12 programs with improved facilities.

name: Texas A&M University
notes: Largest enrollment in the country. Aggie culture is deeply passionate. Women's program has made multiple deep NCAA runs.

name: University of Georgia
notes: Athens is one of the great college towns. UGA Women won the SEC tournament in 2024. Strong southern recruiting base.

name: University of Arkansas
notes: Women's program is one of the best in the SEC with multiple NCAA title runs. Fayetteville is a hidden gem for college life.

name: University of Alabama
notes: Strong football culture but soccer is growing. Good scholarship money available in the SEC. Large, energetic campus.

name: University of Tennessee
notes: Tennessee Women have a strong recruiting base in the Southeast. Beautiful Knoxville location with passionate fan support.

name: Auburn University
notes: Classic SEC football culture with growing soccer programs. War Eagle atmosphere is electric. Strong Southeast recruiting.

name: University of South Carolina
notes: Women's program has reached the Elite Eight multiple times. Mark Berson is one of the most respected men's coaches in college soccer history.

name: Southern Methodist University
notes: Private school in upscale Dallas with elite facilities. ACC addition elevated both programs. Strong alumni network in business and energy sectors.

name: University of Denver
notes: Private university with a top-ranked business school. Denver's outdoor lifestyle is a major draw. Multiple Summit League titles.

name: University of Central Florida
notes: Largest university in the US by enrollment. Orlando location is great for recruiting. Women's coach is a 2x Olympic gold medalist.

name: Elon University
notes: Beautiful boutique university with strong academics. Great option for players who want strong D1 competition without a massive campus.

name: High Point University
notes: Newly renovated campus with top-tier facilities for its size. One of the most beautiful small D1 campuses in the Southeast.

name: James Madison University
notes: Fun college town in the Shenandoah Valley. JMU Women have made multiple NCAA tournament appearances. Spirited campus culture.

name: Belmont University
notes: Nashville-based university known for music and entrepreneurship. Growing D1 program with a strong campus culture.

name: Appalachian State University
notes: Mountain campus in Boone with a unique identity. Sun Belt soccer is competitive; good D1 opportunity for developmental players.

name: Sacramento State University
notes: California state school with affordable tuition. Big Sky Conference D1 is a solid path to professional ambitions for California recruits.

name: Eastern Washington University
notes: Small-town school with an underdog culture. Good scholarship availability and immediate playing time potential in a competitive Big Sky.

name: University of the Incarnate Word
notes: Catholic university in San Antonio with accessible academic standards. WAC Conference provides real D1 experience with achievable roster spots.

name: UT Rio Grande Valley
notes: South Texas university with an active recruiting pipeline from Mexico and Central America. Immediate impact possible for qualified D1 prospects.

name: Grand Canyon University
notes: Christian school with stunning campus facilities and Phoenix weather. Growing D1 program with strong investment from the university.

name: University of North Texas
notes: Large Texas state school near Dallas. American Athletic Conference provides solid mid-major D1 competition.

name: Lenoir-Rhyne University
notes: One of the premier D2 soccer programs in the South Atlantic Conference. Multiple NCAA tournament appearances and a strong soccer culture.

name: University of Tampa
notes: Consistently one of the top D2 programs nationally. Beautiful Tampa location with year-round warm weather for training.

name: Florida Southern College
notes: Small private school with strong academic and athletic tradition. Beautiful Frank Lloyd Wright campus in central Florida.

name: Rollins College
notes: Liberal arts college in Orlando suburb with one of the prettiest campuses in Florida. Academically stronger than most D2 programs.

name: Barry University
notes: Miami-area school with diverse international recruiting. Soccer is a major part of campus identity at Barry.

name: Eckerd College
notes: Waterfront campus on Tampa Bay. Small liberal arts school with a dedicated soccer program and excellent campus life.

name: Queens University of Charlotte
notes: Charlotte-based school with excellent soccer facilities and city access. One of the rising D2 programs in the Southeast.

name: Wingate University
notes: Small school with a strong soccer identity in the loaded South Atlantic Conference. Competitive D2 program with good scholarship opportunities.

name: Lincoln Memorial University
notes: Small Appalachian school with accessible admission and growing athletic profile. Strong for athletes needing academic flexibility with competitive soccer.

name: Carson-Newman University
notes: Christian liberal arts school in East Tennessee. Affordable and accessible for student-athletes who want a faith-centered environment.

name: University of Montevallo
notes: Small Alabama state school with accessible academic requirements. GSC competition is realistic for athletes needing a lower GPA threshold.

name: Valdosta State University
notes: South Georgia school with solid D2 competition. Warm climate, affordable tuition, and accessible admission standards.

name: Colorado School of Mines
notes: Elite engineering school with one of the highest-GPA soccer rosters in D2. Graduates are highly recruited in STEM industries.

name: Fort Lewis College
notes: Stunning Durango location in the San Juan Mountains. Outdoor lifestyle and adventure culture. High Native American enrollment; free tuition for eligible students.

name: Grand Valley State University
notes: GVSU Women are perennial D2 national title contenders. One of the best D2 programs in the country by consistent performance.

name: Minnesota State University Mankato
notes: Strong NSIC program in southern Minnesota. Solid D2 competition with good scholarship availability for both men and women.

name: St. Cloud State University
notes: Minnesota state school with a solid athletic culture. Good for Midwest recruits seeking D2 with accessible admission.

name: West Chester University
notes: Strong PSAC D2 program near Philadelphia. Competitive conference with multiple nationally ranked programs. Good in-state tuition rates.

name: Shippensburg University
notes: State school with affordable tuition and accessible D2 competition. Good option for PA-area recruits needing lower academic thresholds.

name: Seton Hill University
notes: Small Catholic school near Pittsburgh with a personal, supportive environment. Good for players who want immediate impact at the D2 level.

name: Rockhurst University
notes: Jesuit school in Kansas City with a strong soccer tradition. MIAA is one of the most competitive D2 conferences in the country.

name: Drury University
notes: Drury Men are one of the most successful D2 programs historically. Both programs are consistent MIAA contenders.

name: Missouri S&T
notes: Engineering-focused school where student-athletes are academically serious. GPA floor is higher here than most D2 programs.

name: University of Central Missouri
notes: Missouri state school with affordable tuition. MIAA competition is strong even at this level; prepares players well for potential grad transfers.

name: Midwestern State University
notes: North Texas school with solid LSC competition. Lower academic requirements make this accessible for athletes who are borderline academically.

name: West Texas A&M University
notes: Panhandle Texas school with affordable tuition and accessible admission. LSC conference offers D2-level competition without elite academic demands.

name: Eastern New Mexico University
notes: New Mexico state school with one of the lowest GPA thresholds in D2. Good option for athletes who need academic support and a fresh start.

name: University of Findlay
notes: G-MAC school in northwest Ohio. Both programs are consistent conference contenders and NCAA tournament regulars.

name: University of Indianapolis
notes: Private school in Indianapolis with a strong athletic culture. City access provides internship and career opportunities in a major Midwest hub.

name: University of North Georgia
notes: Military college in the North Georgia mountains. Unique culture with strong leadership development alongside competitive soccer.

name: Cal Poly Pomona
notes: California state school in the LA metro area. CCAA is one of the most competitive D2 conferences in the West. No athletic scholarships but Cal Grant eligibility.

name: Harding University
notes: Christian university with strong values culture. GAC competition is solid for D2. Good fit for players who want a faith-based environment.

name: Williams College
notes: One of the top liberal arts colleges in the country. NESCAC soccer is elite for D3. Exceptional alumni network and career outcomes.

name: Amherst College
notes: Elite liberal arts college with need-blind admissions. Opens doors that few other schools can match post-graduation.

name: Middlebury College
notes: Middlebury Women have won multiple NESCAC championships. Beautiful Vermont campus with world-class language programs.

name: Tufts University
notes: Tufts is just north of Boston with strong pre-med and international relations programs. A top NESCAC program annually.

name: Bowdoin College
notes: One of the most selective liberal arts schools in the country. Coastal Maine campus with a strong sailing and outdoor culture.

name: Hamilton College
notes: All open curriculum liberal arts college. Strong writing program and exceptional student independence. Upstate NY campus with classic beauty.

name: Bates College
notes: Test-optional elite liberal arts school. Strong athletics culture balanced with rigorous academics. Beautiful Maine campus.

name: Colby College
notes: Stunning campus in Maine. Strong environmental studies and pre-law tracks. NESCAC competition is some of the best in D3.

name: Wesleyan University
notes: Elite liberal arts university in Connecticut. Strong social sciences and arts culture. Need-blind admissions for domestic students.

name: Trinity College
notes: Urban campus in Hartford with strong alumni connections to finance and law. Highly competitive NESCAC program.

name: Emory University
notes: Top pre-med and research university in Atlanta. UAA soccer is among the best D3 competition nationally. Exceptional medical and career network.

name: Washington University in St. Louis
notes: Top-10 national university. WashU soccer is D3 royalty — multiple national titles for both programs. Requires the highest academic credentials.

name: Carnegie Mellon University
notes: World-class STEM and arts university. CMU's network in tech and engineering is unmatched. Ideal for athletes pursuing STEM careers.

name: Brandeis University
notes: Research university west of Boston. Strong social sciences and pre-law pipeline. UAA conference is extremely competitive for D3.

name: New York University
notes: Playing D3 soccer in New York City is a unique experience. NYU's location and alumni network in media, finance, and the arts is incomparable.

name: University of Chicago
notes: One of the nation's top research universities. Extremely rigorous academics — requires athletes who can excel in both. Prestigious career outcomes.

name: University of Rochester
notes: Strong music, science, and engineering programs. UAA competes at the highest D3 level. Rochester's medical school pipeline is exceptional.

name: Messiah University
notes: Messiah has won more NCAA D3 national championships than any program in history. Christian school with an extraordinary soccer culture.

name: Franklin & Marshall College
notes: Academically elite liberal arts college in Pennsylvania's Lancaster County. Centennial Conference is one of the most competitive D3 leagues.

name: Dickinson College
notes: Strong international studies and sustainability programs. Centennial Conference guarantees excellent D3 competition with elite academic peers.

name: Gettysburg College
notes: Historic campus with a strong liberal arts tradition. Both programs are regular Centennial Conference contenders.

name: Muhlenberg College
notes: Liberal arts college in the Lehigh Valley with a strong pre-med and theater pipeline. Active soccer alumni community.

name: Wheaton College (IL)
notes: Elite Christian liberal arts college. CCIW is one of the best D3 conferences in the Midwest. Strong community and discipleship culture.

name: Denison University
notes: Top NCAC liberal arts school. Strong alumni engagement and career services. Both programs compete at the top of one of the most athletic D3 conferences.

name: Ohio Wesleyan University
notes: Jay Martin is one of the winningest coaches in D3 men's history. Strong liberal arts education with good financial aid packages.

name: Pomona-Pitzer Colleges
notes: One of the most selective colleges in the country. Claremont Colleges consortium gives access to five schools' resources. Need-blind admissions.

name: Chapman University
notes: Southern California private school with excellent film and business programs. SCIAC is competitive with strong academic institutions throughout.

name: Calvin University
notes: Christian Reformed university with a serious soccer culture. MIAA has multiple nationally ranked D3 programs. Strong in education, nursing, and engineering.

name: Hope College
notes: Beautiful Holland, MI campus on Lake Macatawa. MIAA tradition is strong and both programs compete well nationally.

name: Case Western Reserve University
notes: Top engineering and pre-med university. Strong Cleveland medical ecosystem. UAA conference is elite-level D3 competition.

name: Ithaca College
notes: Located on a stunning hill above Cayuga Lake. Communications and physical therapy programs are nationally ranked. Great D3 soccer culture.

name: Rowan University
notes: Large NJ public school with a competitive D3 program. NJAC soccer is among the best in the region. Affordable in-state tuition.

name: SUNY Cortland
notes: Top sport management and physical education programs. SUNYAC competition is solid for D3 at a very affordable price point.

name: Mary Hardin-Baylor University
notes: UMHB is one of the most successful D3 programs in the country. Multiple national championship appearances. Strong Christian culture in central Texas.

name: Hardin-Simmons University
notes: Christian liberal arts university in West Texas. ASC competition includes UMHB so the conference is nationally relevant at D3.

name: Lycoming College
notes: Small Pennsylvania liberal arts college with good academic support. MAC Conference offers solid regional D3 competition.

name: Lindsey Wilson College
notes: Arguably the best NAIA soccer program in the country. Multiple national titles. Recruits internationally with a winning culture and accessible admission.

name: Grand View University
notes: One of the top NAIA men's programs in the country. Multiple national championships. Strong international and domestic recruiting.

name: Morningside University
notes: GPAC is one of the best NAIA conferences. Both programs compete nationally. Great scholarship money and personal attention from coaches.

name: Keiser University
notes: South Florida school with strong international recruiting. Both programs are national NAIA contenders. Warm weather year-round training environment.

name: Oklahoma City University
notes: Private university in OKC with a strong performing arts culture alongside athletics. Accessible admission with merit scholarships available.

name: Briar Cliff University
notes: Small Catholic school competing in the loaded GPAC conference. Strong community feel and personal relationships with coaching staff.

name: Northwestern College (IA)
notes: Christian college in northwest Iowa competing in GPAC. Tight-knit community with strong values culture. Good scholarship opportunities.

name: Doane University
notes: Small Nebraska liberal arts school. GPAC competition is excellent preparation for any athlete with professional aspirations.

name: Concordia University (NE)
notes: Lutheran liberal arts school in Nebraska. Both programs regularly qualify for NAIA national tournament. Faith-centered academic environment.

name: William Carey University
notes: Mississippi Baptist school with very accessible admission. SSAC provides competitive NAIA soccer without elite academic demands.

name: Friends University
notes: Quaker-founded school in Wichita with very accessible admission. Good scholarship availability for student-athletes in the Sooner Athletic Conference.

name: Ottawa University (KS)
notes: Small Baptist school in eastern Kansas. Accessible admission and scholarship money available. Rebuilding programs with immediate playing time potential.

name: The Master's University
notes: Christian school in Southern California with GSAC competition. Southern California location provides a great lifestyle alongside competitive soccer.

name: Columbia College (MO)
notes: Small Missouri school with good scholarship availability. College town atmosphere in the shadow of Mizzou with accessible academic requirements.

name: Indiana Tech
notes: Technical university in Fort Wayne with accessible admission. WHAC conference is competitive and the school invests in athletic programs.

name: Tyler Junior College
notes: Elite JUCO program that has sent numerous players to D1. Best pipeline JUCO in Texas. If you need to prove yourself before a 4-year offer, this is it.

name: Hutchinson Community College
notes: Kansas JUCO with strong soccer tradition. KJCCC is one of the best junior college conferences nationally. Affordable and effective springboard to 4-year programs.

name: Barton County Community College
notes: One of the top JUCO programs in the country. Barton has placed numerous players at D1 and D2 programs. Excellent stepping stone for overlooked talent.

name: Seward County Community College
notes: Strong Kansas JUCO program. KJCCC competition prepares you well for 4-year college soccer. Very affordable and accessible.

name: Cowley College
notes: Kansas JUCO with solid competition. Good option for athletes needing to rebuild their academic record while staying competitive.

name: Trinity Valley Community College
notes: East Texas JUCO powerhouse. Trinity Valley has produced D1 transfer prospects consistently. Strong international recruiting pipeline.

name: Northeastern Oklahoma A&M College
notes: NEO A&M is a top NJCAA program in Oklahoma with a history of producing transfer players for D1 and D2 programs.

name: San Jacinto College
notes: Large Houston-area JUCO with a diverse student body. Strong soccer tradition with good pipeline to 4-year Texas programs.

name: Cloud County Community College
notes: Kansas JUCO in small-town Concordia. Lowest barrier to entry in college soccer. Good for athletes who need eligibility restored or a fresh academic start.

name: Harvard University
notes: No athletic scholarships (Ivy rule), but generous need-based aid. Highly competitive admissions — coach support helps but doesn't override academic floor. Strong international recruiting.

name: Yale University
notes: Ivy League: need-based aid only. Reichlin Field hosts both programs. Men's program made a strong NCAA tournament run in 2019; women's program competes for Ivy titles.

name: Princeton University
notes: Princeton women have won multiple Ivy League titles; men have produced MLS draftees. No athletic scholarships, but strong financial aid for accepted players.

name: University of Pennsylvania
notes: Ivy League program in an urban setting. Penn women have been Ivy contenders; the men's program has steady NCAA appearances. Wharton attracts business-minded recruits.

name: Cornell University
notes: Largest Ivy by undergrad. Cornell men's program has had steady Ivy success and produced MLS players. Need-based aid only.

name: Columbia University
notes: NYC-based Ivy. Smaller athletic facilities than peers but elite academics. Need-based aid only.

name: Dartmouth College
notes: Smallest Ivy. Both programs have made deep NCAA runs. Need-based aid only; tight-knit campus and strong alumni network.

name: Brown University
notes: Open curriculum and strong Ivy soccer programs. Brown women have produced national team players. Need-based aid only.

name: University of Florida
notes: Florida women have won an NCAA title (1998) and produced multiple USWNT players. SEC competition with full athletic scholarships. Florida does not field men's varsity soccer.

name: Vanderbilt University
notes: Vanderbilt combines elite SEC academics with competitive women's soccer. Need-blind admissions and strong financial aid. No men's varsity program.

name: University of Kentucky
notes: Kentucky men's soccer competes in C-USA (the SEC has no men's soccer); women's is full SEC. Both programs have made NCAA tournament appearances.

name: Mississippi State University
notes: SEC women's program with full athletic scholarships. Lower academic floor than Vanderbilt or Florida. No men's program.

name: University of Mississippi
notes: Ole Miss women's soccer competes in the SEC. Charming Oxford campus with strong school spirit. No men's varsity soccer.

name: University of Missouri
notes: Mizzou women regularly compete for SEC tournament berths. Large flagship state school. No men's varsity program.

name: Louisiana State University
notes: LSU women's soccer in the SEC. Strong athletic culture and warm-weather year-round training. No men's program.

name: Michigan State University
notes: Michigan State men's soccer historically strong; women's program competes in the Big Ten. Large public flagship with full athletic resources.

name: University of Minnesota
notes: Minnesota women's soccer competes in the Big Ten. Strong academic flagship in a major metro. No men's varsity program.

name: University of Nebraska
notes: Nebraska women's soccer in the Big Ten. Big-time athletics culture and one of the largest stadium fanbases in college sports. No men's varsity program.

name: University of Illinois
notes: Illinois women's soccer competes in the Big Ten. Strong engineering and CS programs attract academic recruits. No men's varsity program.

name: University of Iowa
notes: Iowa women's soccer in the Big Ten. Strong overall athletic department; warm campus community. No men's varsity program.

name: University of Southern California
notes: USC women won the 2007 NCAA title and remain a top program. Big Ten competition starting 2024. No men's varsity program. Elite academics + LA recruiting hotbed.

name: University of Oregon
notes: Oregon women's soccer in the Big Ten. Top-tier athletic facilities (Nike-funded). No men's varsity program.

name: Oregon State University
notes: Oregon State competes in the WCC for soccer (after Pac-12 dissolution). Both men's and women's varsity. Strong athletic infrastructure.

name: Oklahoma State University
notes: Oklahoma State women's soccer in the Big 12. Solid mid-tier program with full scholarships. No men's varsity program.

name: University of Oklahoma
notes: Oklahoma women's soccer; recently moved to SEC. Strong school athletics culture. No men's varsity program.

name: University of Houston
notes: Houston women's soccer in the Big 12. Large urban university with diverse student body. No men's varsity program.

name: Kansas State University
notes: K-State women's soccer in the Big 12. Smaller-town college experience. No men's varsity program.

name: Iowa State University
notes: Iowa State women's soccer competes in the Big 12. Strong engineering academics. No men's varsity program.

name: University of Cincinnati
notes: Cincinnati men's and women's soccer; recently moved to Big 12. Urban campus with strong co-op/internship culture.

name: University of Arizona
notes: Arizona women's soccer in the Big 12. Warm-weather training year-round. No men's varsity program.

name: Arizona State University
notes: ASU women's soccer in the Big 12. One of the largest enrollments in the country. No men's varsity program.

name: University of Utah
notes: Utah women's soccer in the Big 12. Strong academic state flagship with mountain recreation access. No men's varsity program.

name: University of Miami
notes: Miami women's soccer in the ACC. Private university with strong academics; warm-weather year-round training. No men's varsity program.

name: Syracuse University
notes: Syracuse men's soccer won the 2022 NCAA national title. Both programs compete in the ACC. Strong communications/Newhouse academics.

name: University of California, Berkeley
notes: Cal women's soccer in the ACC. Top public university by academics; admissions is selective beyond athletics. No men's varsity program.

name: Saint Louis University
notes: SLU men's soccer is the most decorated program in NCAA history (10 national titles, all pre-2000). Still a strong A-10 program. Women's program is competitive in the A-10.

name: Marquette University
notes: Marquette competes in the Big East for both programs. Jesuit private with strong business/health science academics.

name: University of Connecticut
notes: UConn men have multiple NCAA Final Four runs and produced numerous MLS players. Women's program competes in the Big East.

name: University of San Diego
notes: USD men have made multiple deep NCAA tournament runs. Beautiful coastal campus, Catholic-affiliated. Both programs compete in the WCC.

name: Saint Mary's College of California
notes: Small Catholic liberal arts college near Berkeley. Both programs compete in the WCC. Tight-knit campus community.

name: University of Akron
notes: Akron men's soccer is one of the top programs in NCAA — 2010 national champion, perennial College Cup contender. The men's program produces elite MLS draft picks.

name: College of William & Mary
notes: Top public liberal arts college (the 'Public Ivy'). Both programs are competitive in the CAA. Selective admissions even with athletic recommendation.

name: University of South Florida
notes: USF competes in the American Athletic Conference. Both programs have NCAA tournament histories. Year-round Florida training.

name: Loyola Marymount University
notes: LMU is a Jesuit university in West LA. Both programs compete in the WCC. Strong film/business programs and beach-adjacent campus.

name: University of San Francisco
notes: USF men's soccer has 5 NCAA national titles (all pre-1980). Still produces MLS players. Both programs compete in the WCC.

name: University of New Mexico
notes: UNM men have a strong NCAA tournament history including a College Cup appearance. Affordable in-state tuition and warm Southwest climate.

name: Liberty University
notes: Liberty is a Christian university with strong faith-based culture. Both programs compete in C-USA after moving up from the ASun.

name: Old Dominion University
notes: ODU women's soccer has NCAA tournament history. Coastal Virginia campus with strong international student community.

name: Coastal Carolina University
notes: Coastal Carolina men's soccer has been a Sun Belt power (multiple NCAA tournament appearances). Beach-adjacent campus near Myrtle Beach.

name: UC Santa Barbara
notes: UCSB men's soccer won the 2006 NCAA national title and is consistently a top-25 program. Beach campus, strong academics, top California public school.

name: UC Irvine
notes: UC Irvine competes in the Big West. Strong UC academics with growing athletic department investment.

name: Saint Joseph's University
notes: Jesuit university in Philadelphia. Both programs compete in the A-10. Strong business school.

name: Davidson College
notes: Davidson is one of the most academically selective small D1 schools. Both programs compete in the A-10. Honor code culture and tight-knit campus.

name: Virginia Commonwealth University
notes: VCU men's soccer has been an A-10 power with NCAA tournament appearances. Urban Richmond campus with strong arts/design programs.

name: Florida Gulf Coast University
notes: FGCU competes in the ASun. Both programs have made NCAA tournament appearances. Warm SW Florida campus.

name: Creighton University
notes: Creighton men's soccer is a Big East power and frequent NCAA tournament participant. Jesuit private with strong pre-med pipeline.

name: Providence College
notes: Catholic Dominican college in New England. Both programs compete in the Big East.

name: St. John's University
notes: St. John's competes in the Big East. Catholic Vincentian university in NYC. Strong men's soccer history.

name: Johns Hopkins University
notes: One of the top academic D3 programs in the country. Both soccer programs are competitive in the Centennial Conference. No athletic scholarships, but strong financial aid. Top-ranked pre-med and engineering.

name: Haverford College
notes: Haverford is one of the most academically selective small liberal arts colleges. Quaker-rooted honor code culture. Centennial Conference soccer.

name: Swarthmore College
notes: Top-3 national liberal arts college (US News). Honors program is among the most rigorous in the country. Centennial Conference soccer.

name: Washington College
notes: Small liberal arts college on the Chesapeake. Centennial Conference soccer. Strong creative writing tradition.

name: Claremont-Mudd-Scripps
notes: Combined athletic program of Claremont McKenna, Harvey Mudd, and Scripps. CMS men have won NCAA D3 national titles. Elite academics across all three colleges.

name: Occidental College
notes: Top liberal arts college in LA. Strong UN/diplomatic studies tradition (alumni include Obama). SCIAC soccer.

name: California Institute of Technology
notes: Among the most academically selective universities in the world. Athletics is recreational by elite-college standards but the SCIAC schedule is real D3. STEM-only academic environment.

name: Rochester Institute of Technology
notes: Strong tech/engineering academics. Liberty League soccer. Both programs are competitive in conference play.

name: Vassar College
notes: Top-tier liberal arts college (originally women's only, coed since 1969). Strong arts/humanities. Liberty League soccer.

name: Skidmore College
notes: Strong liberal arts college upstate New York. Both programs are competitive in the Liberty League. 'Creative Thought Matters' culture.

name: St. Olaf College
notes: Lutheran-affiliated liberal arts college. Strong music conservatory. MIAC soccer with both programs traditionally competitive.

name: Carleton College
notes: Top-10 national liberal arts college (US News). Trimester academic calendar. MIAC soccer; both programs are competitive.

name: Macalester College
notes: Twin Cities-based liberal arts college with strong international focus and political science tradition. MIAC soccer.

name: Gustavus Adolphus College
notes: Lutheran-affiliated liberal arts college. Strong soccer tradition; both programs are MIAC-competitive.

name: Kenyon College
notes: Top liberal arts college with a famed creative writing program. Hilltop rural Ohio campus. NCAC soccer.

name: Wittenberg University
notes: Lutheran-affiliated liberal arts college in Ohio. NCAC soccer with both programs competitive.

name: DePauw University
notes: Top liberal arts college in rural Indiana. Strong Greek life and journalism programs. NCAC soccer.

name: University of Lynchburg
notes: Lynchburg men's soccer is among the top D3 programs nationally with multiple Final Four appearances. ODAC competition. Strong recruiting culture.

name: Washington and Lee University
notes: Top-tier liberal arts/law college in rural Virginia. Highly selective. ODAC soccer; both programs competitive.

name: Massachusetts Institute of Technology
notes: World-class STEM university. NEWMAC soccer is competitive but academics dominate the experience. Need-based aid only.

name: Stevens Institute of Technology
notes: Top engineering tech school across the river from Manhattan. Strong men's soccer with NCAA tournament appearances. MAC Freedom Conference.

name: Christopher Newport University
notes: Coastal Virginia public liberal arts university. Strong men's soccer program competing in the C2C.

name: Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute
notes: Top engineering institute in upstate NY. Liberty League soccer.

name: Connecticut College
notes: NESCAC liberal arts college on the CT shore. Both programs compete in one of the strongest D3 conferences.

name: Loras College
notes: Loras men's soccer is a national D3 power (multiple Final Fours). Catholic liberal arts college. American Rivers Conference.

name: Trinity University
notes: Top liberal arts college in the South. Trinity men's soccer has won multiple NCAA D3 national titles. SAA conference.

name: Otterbein University
notes: Suburban Columbus liberal arts college. OAC soccer with strong men's and women's history.

name: Wabash College
notes: All-male liberal arts college. NCAC soccer (men's only). Strong gentleman's-rule honor culture and tight alumni network.

name: Smith College
notes: Top women's college (one of the Seven Sisters). NEWMAC soccer (women's only). Strong feminist tradition and Mountain Day culture.

name: Bryn Mawr College
notes: Top women's college (Seven Sisters). Centennial Conference soccer (women's only). Honor code culture and bi-college consortium with Haverford.

name: Saint Anselm College
notes: Catholic Benedictine college in NH. Both programs are competitive in the NE10. Strong men's soccer history.

name: Franklin Pierce University
notes: Franklin Pierce has been an NE10 soccer power with multiple NCAA D2 final four runs. Small NH campus.

name: Bentley University
notes: Top D2 business-focused university near Boston. Strong NE10 soccer programs.

name: Le Moyne College
notes: Catholic Jesuit college transitioning some sports to D1. Strong NE10 soccer history.

name: Western Washington University
notes: WWU is a top D2 soccer program, especially women's (multiple NCAA Final Fours and a national title). Beautiful PNW campus.

name: Seattle Pacific University
notes: SPU men's soccer has won D2 national titles. Christian university in Seattle. GNAC competition.

name: Lewis University
notes: Catholic university in Chicago suburbs. Strong men's soccer history in the GLVC. Aviation programs known nationally.

name: Truman State University
notes: Top public liberal arts university in the Midwest (the 'Public Ivy of Missouri'). GLVC soccer.

name: Saint Martin's University
notes: Catholic Benedictine university in WA. GNAC soccer.

name: Northwest Nazarene University
notes: Christian university in southern Idaho. GNAC soccer.

name: The College of Saint Rose
notes: Catholic college in NY's capital region. NE10 soccer.

name: AIC (American International College)
notes: Diverse private university in Springfield. NE10 soccer with strong international recruiting.

name: Mercyhurst University
notes: Catholic university in Erie, PA. PSAC soccer with both programs traditionally competitive.

name: Ashland University
notes: Private university in central Ohio. G-MAC soccer.

name: Tiffin University
notes: Tiffin men's soccer competes for G-MAC titles. Affordable small private school in Ohio.
